metadata.title: Ambry: LinkedIn’s Scalable Geo-Distributed Object Store
metadata.titleTranslation: Ambry：LinkedIn 的可扩展地理分布式对象存储
metadata.source: Noghabi 等 - 2016 - Ambry LinkedIn's Scalable Geo-Distributed Object Store.pdf

sections.title: Abstract
sections.originalMarkdown: The infrastructure beneath a worldwide social network has to continually serve billions of variable-sized media objects such as photos, videos, and audio clips. These objects must be stored and served with low latency and high throughput by a system that is geo-distributed, highly scalable, and load-balanced. Existing file systems and object stores face several challenges when serving such large objects. We present Ambry, a production-quality system for storing large immutable data (called blobs). Ambry is designed in a decentralized way and leverages techniques such as logical blob grouping, asynchronous replication, rebalancing mechanisms, zero-cost failure detection, and OS caching. Ambry has been running in LinkedIn’s production environment for the past 2 years, serving up to 10K requests per second across more than 400 million users. Our experimental evaluation reveals that Ambry offers high efficiency (utilizing up to 88% of the network bandwidth), low latency (less than 50 ms latency for a 1 MB object), and load balancing (improving imbalance of request rate among disks by 8x-10x).
sections.translationMarkdown: 全球性社交网络的底层基础设施必须持续服务数十亿个大小各异的媒体对象，例如照片、视频和音频片段。一个地理分布、高度可扩展且负载均衡的系统必须以低延迟和高吞吐量存储并提供这些对象。现有文件系统和对象存储在服务此类大型对象时面临若干挑战。本文提出 Ambry，这是一个用于存储大型不可变数据（称为 blob）的生产级系统。Ambry 采用去中心化设计，并利用 blob 逻辑分组、异步复制、重平衡机制、零成本故障检测和操作系统缓存等技术。Ambry 已在 LinkedIn 的生产环境中运行两年，为超过 4 亿用户提供服务，每秒最多处理 10K 个请求。实验评估表明，Ambry 具有很高的效率（网络带宽利用率最高可达 88%）、较低的延迟（1 MB 对象的延迟低于 50 ms）以及良好的负载均衡能力（将磁盘间请求率的不均衡程度改善 8–10 倍）。

sections.title: 1. INTRODUCTION
sections.originalMarkdown:
During the past decade, social networks have become popular communication channels worldwide. Hundreds of millions of users continually upload and view billions of diverse massive media objects, from photos and videos to documents. These large media objects, called blobs, are uploaded once, frequently accessed from all around the world, never modified, and rarely deleted. LinkedIn, as a global large-scale social network company, has faced the need for a geographically distributed system that stores and retrieves these read-heavy blobs in an efficient and scalable manner.

Handling blobs poses a number of unique challenges. First, due to diversity in media types, blob sizes vary significantly from tens of KBs (e.g., profile pictures) to a few GBs (e.g., videos). The system needs to store both massive blobs and a large number of small blobs efficiently. Second, there is an ever-growing number of blobs that need to be stored and served. Currently, LinkedIn serves more than 800 million put and get operations per day (over 120 TB in size). In the past 12 months, the request rate has almost doubled, from 5k requests/s to 9.5k requests/s. This rapid growth in requests magnifies the necessity for a linearly scalable system (with low overhead). Third, the variability in workload and cluster expansions can create unbalanced load, degrading the latency and throughput of the system. This creates a need for load-balancing. Finally, users expect the uploading process to be fast, durable, and highly available. When a user uploads a blob, all his/her friends from all around the globe should be able to see the blob with very low latency, even if parts of the internal infrastructure fail. To provide these properties, data has to be reliably replicated across the globe in multiple datacenters, while maintaining low latency for each request.

LinkedIn had its own home-grown solution called Media Server, built using network attached storage filers (for file storage), Oracle database (for metadata), and Solaris boxes. Media Server had multiple drawbacks. It faced CPU and IO spikes caused by numerous metadata operations for small objects, was not horizontally scalable, and was very expensive. Given that LinkedIn was scaling rapidly and the future web content will be largely dominated by media, we needed to find a replacement.

Several systems have been designed for handling a large amount of data, but none of them satisfactorily meet the requirements and scale LinkedIn needs. There has been extensive research into distributed file systems [10,16,22,24,28]. These systems have a number of limitations when used for storing blobs, as pointed out by [3, 11]. For instance, the hierarchical directory structure and rich metadata are an overkill for a blob store and impose unnecessary additional overhead.

Many key value stores [2,5,8,14] have also been designed for storing a large number of objects. Although these systems can handle many small objects, they are not optimized for storing large objects (tens of MBs to GBs). Further, they impose extra overhead for providing consistency guarantees while these are typically not needed for immutable data. Some examples of these overheads include using vector clocks, conflict resolution mechanism, logging, and central coordinators.

A few systems have been designed specifically for large immutable objects including Facebook’s Haystack [3] along with f4 [18] and Twitter’s Blob Store [27]. However, these systems do not resolve load imbalance, especially when cluster expansions occur.

In this paper we present Ambry, a production-quality system designed specifically for diverse large and small immutable data with read-heavy traffic, where data is written once, and read many times (>95% read traffic). Ambry is designed with four main goals in mind:

1. **Low Latency and High Throughput:** The system needs to serve a large number of requests per second in a timely fashion, while working on cheap commodity hardware (e.g., HDDs). In order to reach this goal, Ambry utilizes a number of techniques including exploiting the OS cache, using zero copy when reading data from disk to network, chunking data along with retrieving/storing chunks in parallel from multiple nodes, providing configurable polices for the number of replicas to write and read, and zero-cost failure detection mechanisms (Sections 2.3, 4.2, and 4.3).
2. **Geo-Distributed Operation:** Blobs have to be replicated in other geographically distributed datacenters for high durability and availability, even in the presence of failures. To achieve low latency and high throughput in this geo-distributed setting, Ambry is designed as a decentralized multi-master system where data can be written to or read from any of the replicas. Additionally, it uses asynchronous writes that write data to the closest datacenter and asynchronously replicate to other datacenter(s). Also, for higher availability, it uses proxy requests that forward requests to other datacenters when the data is not replicated in the current datacenter yet (Sections 2.3 and 4.2).
3. **Scalability:** With the ever-growing amount of data, the system has to scale out efficiently with low overhead. To achieve this goal, Ambry makes three main design choices. First, Ambry separates the logical placement of blobs from their physical placement, allowing it to change the physical placement transparently from the logical placement. Second, Ambry is designed as a completely decentralized system, with no manager/master. Third, Ambry uses on-disk segmented indexing along with Bloom filters and an in-memory cache of the latest segment, allowing for scalable and efficient indexing of blobs. (Section 4.3).
4. **Load Balancing:** The system has to stay balanced in spite of growth. Ambry uses chunking of large blobs along with a random selection approach to remain balanced in a static cluster, and a re-balancing mechanism to return to a balanced state whenever cluster expansion occurs (Section 3).

Ambry has successfully been in production for the last 24 months, across four datacenters, serving more than 400 million users. Our experimental results show that Ambry reaches high throughput (reaching up to 88% of the network bandwidth) and low latency (serving 1 MB blobs in less than 50 ms), works efficiently across multiple geo-distributed datacenters, and improves the imbalance among disks by a factor of 8x-10x while moving minimal data.
sections.translationMarkdown:
在过去十年中，社交网络已成为全球流行的沟通渠道。数亿用户不断上传和查看数十亿个形式各异的大型媒体对象，涵盖照片、视频和文档。这些大型媒体对象称为 blob：它们只上传一次，经常被世界各地访问，从不修改，也很少删除。作为一家全球性的大规模社交网络公司，LinkedIn 需要一个地理分布式系统，以高效且可扩展的方式存储和检索这些以读为主的 blob。

处理 blob 带来了一些独特挑战。第一，由于媒体类型多样，blob 大小差异显著，从几十 KB（例如个人头像）到数 GB（例如视频）不等。系统需要同时高效存储巨型 blob 和大量小型 blob。第二，需要存储和服务的 blob 数量不断增长。目前，LinkedIn 每天处理超过 8 亿次 put 和 get 操作（总大小超过 120 TB）。过去 12 个月中，请求率几乎翻了一番，从 5k 请求/s 增长到 9.5k 请求/s。请求的快速增长进一步凸显了对可线性扩展且开销较低的系统的需求。第三，工作负载变化和集群扩容可能造成负载不均衡，进而降低系统的延迟和吞吐量表现，因此系统需要负载均衡。最后，用户希望上传过程快速、持久且高度可用。当用户上传一个 blob 后，即使部分内部基础设施发生故障，其世界各地的所有朋友也应能够以很低的延迟看到该 blob。为了提供这些特性，数据必须在全球多个数据中心之间可靠复制，同时保持每个请求的低延迟。

LinkedIn 曾有一个自研方案 Media Server，它使用网络附加存储设备（用于文件存储）、Oracle 数据库（用于元数据）和 Solaris 服务器构建。Media Server 有多个缺点：针对小对象的大量元数据操作会造成 CPU 和 I/O 峰值；系统无法水平扩展；成本也非常高。鉴于 LinkedIn 正在快速扩张，而未来的 Web 内容将主要由媒体主导，我们需要寻找替代方案。

已有若干系统被设计用于处理大量数据，但没有一个能够令人满意地满足 LinkedIn 所需的要求和规模。分布式文件系统领域已有大量研究 [10,16,22,24,28]。正如文献 [3, 11] 指出的那样，这些系统用于存储 blob 时存在一些局限。例如，分层目录结构和丰富的元数据对于 blob 存储而言功能过度，并会引入不必要的额外开销。

许多 key-value store [2,5,8,14] 也被设计用于存储大量对象。尽管这些系统能够处理许多小对象，但它们并未针对大型对象（几十 MB 到数 GB）优化。此外，它们为了提供一致性保证而引入额外开销，而不可变数据通常并不需要这些保证。此类开销包括使用 vector clock、冲突解决机制、日志记录和中央协调器。

还有少数系统专为大型不可变对象设计，包括 Facebook 的 Haystack [3] 与 f4 [18]，以及 Twitter 的 Blob Store [27]。然而，这些系统并未解决负载不均衡问题，尤其是在集群扩容时。

本文提出 Ambry，这是一个生产级系统，专门面向读密集流量下大小各异的不可变数据，其中数据只写入一次、读取多次（读流量 >95%）。Ambry 的设计围绕四个主要目标展开：

1. **低延迟与高吞吐量：** 系统需要在廉价的通用硬件（例如 HDD）上及时服务每秒大量请求。为实现这一目标，Ambry 采用了多种技术，包括利用操作系统缓存；从磁盘向网络读取数据时使用 zero copy；将数据分块，并从多个节点并行检索或存储这些 chunk；提供可配置策略以控制读写的副本数；以及采用零成本故障检测机制（第 2.3、4.2 和 4.3 节）。
2. **地理分布式运行：** 即使发生故障，也必须将 blob 复制到其他地理分布的数据中心，以提供高持久性和高可用性。为在这种地理分布环境中实现低延迟和高吞吐量，Ambry 被设计为去中心化的 multi-master 系统，可从任意副本写入或读取数据。此外，它采用异步写入：先将数据写到最近的数据中心，再异步复制到其他数据中心。为了提高可用性，当数据尚未复制到当前数据中心时，它还使用 proxy request 将请求转发到其他数据中心（第 2.3 和 4.2 节）。
3. **可扩展性：** 随着数据量持续增长，系统必须以较低开销高效地横向扩展。为此，Ambry 做出了三个主要设计选择。第一，Ambry 将 blob 的逻辑放置与物理放置分离，使其能够在逻辑放置不变的情况下透明改变物理放置。第二，Ambry 被设计为完全去中心化的系统，不设 manager/master。第三，Ambry 使用磁盘上的分段索引、Bloom filter 和最新 segment 的内存缓存，从而实现可扩展且高效的 blob 索引（第 4.3 节）。
4. **负载均衡：** 尽管系统持续增长，它仍必须保持均衡。Ambry 通过对大型 blob 进行 chunking 并采用随机选择方法，使静态集群保持均衡；当集群扩容时，则通过重平衡机制恢复均衡状态（第 3 节）。

Ambry 已在四个数据中心的生产环境中成功运行了 24 个月，为超过 4 亿用户提供服务。实验结果表明，Ambry 可实现高吞吐量（达到网络带宽的 88%）和低延迟（1 MB blob 的服务延迟低于 50 ms），能够在多个地理分布的数据中心之间高效运行，并且只需移动很少的数据，就能将磁盘之间的不均衡程度改善 8–10 倍。

sections.title: 2. SYSTEM OVERVIEW
sections.originalMarkdown: In this section we discuss the overall design of Ambry including the high-level architecture of the system (Section 2.1), the notion of partition (Section 2.2), and supported operations (Section 2.3).
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节讨论 Ambry 的整体设计，包括系统的高层架构（第 2.1 节）、partition 的概念（第 2.2 节）以及支持的操作（第 2.3 节）。

sections.title: 2.1 Architecture
sections.originalMarkdown:
Ambry is designed as a completely decentralized multi-tenant system across geographically distributed data centers. The overall architecture of Ambry is shown in Figure 1. The system is composed of three main components: Frontends that receive and route requests, Datanodes that store the actual data, and Cluster Managers that maintain the state of the cluster. Each datacenter owns and runs its own set of these components in a decentralized fashion. The Frontends and Datanodes are completely independent of one another, and the Cluster Managers are synchronized using Zookeeper [12]. We provide an overview of each component below (details in Section 4).

**Cluster Manager:** Ambry organizes its data in virtual units called partitions (Section 2.2). A partition is a logical grouping of a number of blobs, implemented as a large replicated file. On creation, partitions are read-write, i.e., immutable blobs are read and new blobs can be added. When a logical partition reaches its capacity, it turns read-only. The Cluster Manager keeps track of the state (read-write/read-only) and location of each partition replica, along with the physical layout of the cluster (nodes and disk placement).

**Frontend:** The Frontends are in charge of receiving and routing requests in a multi-tenant environment. The system serves three request types: put, get, and delete. Popular data is handled by a Content Delivery Network (CDN) layer above Ambry. Frontends receive requests directly from clients or through the CDN (if the data is cached). The Frontends forward a request to the corresponding Datanode(s) and return the response to the client/CDN originating the request.

**Datanode:** Datanodes store and retrieve the actual data. Each Datanode manages a number of disks. For better performance, Datanodes maintain a number of additional data structures including: indexing of blobs, journals and Bloom filters (Section 4.3).
sections.translationMarkdown:
Ambry 被设计为跨地理分布数据中心、完全去中心化的多租户系统。图 1 展示了 Ambry 的整体架构。系统由三个主要组件构成：接收并路由请求的 Frontend、存储实际数据的 Datanode，以及维护集群状态的 Cluster Manager。每个数据中心都以去中心化方式拥有并运行自己的一套组件。Frontend 与 Datanode 彼此完全独立，Cluster Manager 则使用 Zookeeper [12] 保持同步。下面概述各组件（详细内容见第 4 节）。

**Cluster Manager：** Ambry 将数据组织为称作 partition 的虚拟单元（第 2.2 节）。partition 是若干 blob 的逻辑分组，实现为一个大型复制文件。partition 创建时为可读写状态，即可以读取不可变 blob，也可以添加新的 blob。当逻辑 partition 达到容量上限后，它会转为只读。Cluster Manager 记录每个 partition 副本的状态（可读写/只读）和位置，以及集群的物理布局（节点和磁盘放置）。

**Frontend：** Frontend 负责在多租户环境中接收并路由请求。系统支持三种请求类型：put、get 和 delete。热门数据由 Ambry 上层的 Content Delivery Network（CDN）层处理。Frontend 直接从客户端接收请求，或在数据被缓存时通过 CDN 接收请求。Frontend 将请求转发到相应的 Datanode，并把响应返回给发起请求的客户端/CDN。

**Datanode：** Datanode 存储和检索实际数据。每个 Datanode 管理若干磁盘。为了提高性能，Datanode 维护多种额外数据结构，包括 blob 索引、journal 和 Bloom filter（第 4.3 节）。

sections.title: 2.2 Partition
sections.originalMarkdown:
Instead of directly mapping blobs to physical machines, e.g., Chord [26] and CRUSH [29], Ambry randomly groups blobs together into virtual units called partitions. The physical placement of partitions on machines is done in a separate procedure. This decoupling of the logical and physical placement enables transparent data movement (necessary for rebalancing) and avoids immediate rehashing of data during cluster expansion.

A partition is implemented as an append-only log in a pre-allocated large file. Currently, partitions are fixed-size during the life-time of the system[^1]. The partition size should be large enough that the overhead of partitions, i.e., the additional data structures maintained per partition such as indexing, journals, and Bloom filters (Section 4.3), are negligible. On the other hand, the failure recovery and rebuild time should be small. We use 100 GB partitions in our clusters. Since rebuilding is done in parallel from multiple replicas, we found that even 100 GB partitions can be rebuilt in a few minutes.

Blobs are sequentially written to partitions as put and delete entries (Figure 2). Both entries contain a header (storing the offsets of fields in the entry) and a blob id. The blob id is a unique identifier, generated by the Frontend during a put operation, and used during get/delete operations for locating the blob. This id consists of the partition id in which the blob is placed (8 Bytes), followed by a 32 Byte universally unique id (UUID) for the blob. Collisions in blob ids are possible, but very unlikely (the probability is < 2⁻³²⁰). For a collision to occur, two put operations have to generate equal UUIDs and chose similar partitions for the blob. Collisions are handled at the Datanodes by failing the late put request.

Put entries also include predefined properties including: blob size, time-to-live, creation time, and content type. Also, there is an optional map of user defined properties followed by the blob.

In order to offer high availability and fault-tolerance, each partition is replicated on multiple Datanodes. For replica placement, Ambry uses a greedy approach based on disk spaces. This algorithm chooses the disk with the most unallocated space while ensuring constraints such as: 1) not having more than one replica per Datanode and 2) having replicas in multiple data centers. Currently, the number of replicas per partition is configurable by the system administrator. As part of future work, we plan to adaptively change the number of replicas based on the popularity of the partition, and use erasure coding for cold data to even further reduce the replication factor.

On creation, partitions are read-write, serving all operations (put, get and delete). When the partition hits its upper threshold on size (capacity threshold) it becomes read-only, thereafter serving only get and delete operations.

The capacity threshold should be slightly less than the max capacity (80-90%) of the partition for two reasons. First, after becoming read-only, replicas might not be completely in-sync and need free space to catch-up later (because of asynchronous writes). Second, delete requests still append delete entries.

Deletes are similar to put operations, but on an existing blob. By default, deletes result in appending a delete entry (with the delete flag set) for the blob (soft delete). Deleted blobs are periodically cleaned up using an in-place compaction mechanism. After compaction, read-only partitions can become read-write if enough space is freed-up. In the rest of the paper we mainly focus on puts, due to the similarity of delete and put operations.

[^1]: As part of future work we plan to investigate potential improvements by using variable-size partitions.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Ambry 并不像 Chord [26] 和 CRUSH [29] 那样将 blob 直接映射到物理机器，而是随机地将 blob 分组到称为 partition 的虚拟单元中。partition 在机器上的物理放置由另一个独立过程完成。逻辑放置与物理放置的解耦使数据能够透明移动（重平衡所必需），并避免在集群扩容时立即对数据重新哈希。

partition 实现为预分配大文件中的 append-only log。目前，在系统的整个生命周期内，partition 的大小固定[^1]。partition 应足够大，使每个 partition 所维护的索引、journal 和 Bloom filter（第 4.3 节）等额外数据结构带来的开销可以忽略不计；另一方面，故障恢复与重建时间又应尽可能短。我们的集群使用 100 GB 的 partition。由于重建会从多个副本并行执行，我们发现即使是 100 GB 的 partition 也能在几分钟内完成重建。

blob 以 put entry 和 delete entry 的形式顺序写入 partition（图 2）。两种 entry 都包含一个 header（存储 entry 内各字段的偏移）和一个 blob id。blob id 是唯一标识符，由 Frontend 在 put 操作期间生成，并在 get/delete 操作中用于定位 blob。该 id 由 blob 所在 partition 的 id（8 Bytes）以及随后一个 32 Byte 的 blob universally unique id（UUID）组成。blob id 可能发生冲突，但概率极低（概率 < 2⁻³²⁰）。要发生冲突，两次 put 操作必须生成相同的 UUID，并为 blob 选择相同的 partition。Datanode 通过让较晚到达的 put 请求失败来处理冲突。

Put entry 还包括预定义属性：blob 大小、time-to-live、创建时间和内容类型。此外，还可以包含一个可选的用户自定义属性映射，其后是 blob 数据。

为了提供高可用性与容错能力，每个 partition 都会复制到多个 Datanode。对于副本放置，Ambry 使用基于磁盘空间的贪心方法。该算法选择未分配空间最多的磁盘，同时确保以下约束：1）每个 Datanode 上最多只能有一个副本；2）副本分布在多个数据中心。目前，每个 partition 的副本数量可由系统管理员配置。作为未来工作，我们计划根据 partition 的热度自适应改变副本数量，并对冷数据使用 erasure coding，以进一步降低 replication factor。

partition 创建时为可读写状态，可服务全部操作（put、get 和 delete）。当 partition 的大小达到上限阈值（容量阈值）时，它会转为只读，此后仅服务 get 和 delete 操作。

出于两个原因，容量阈值应略低于 partition 的最大容量（为其 80–90%）。第一，partition 变为只读后，各副本可能尚未完全同步，需要留出空间供其稍后追赶（因为采用了异步写入）。第二，delete 请求仍会追加 delete entry。

delete 与 put 类似，但操作对象是已有 blob。默认情况下，delete 会为该 blob 追加一条设置了删除标志的 delete entry（软删除）。系统使用 in-place compaction 机制定期清理已删除的 blob。compaction 后，如果释放了足够空间，只读 partition 可以重新变为可读写。由于 delete 与 put 操作相似，本文余下部分主要关注 put。

[^1]: 作为未来工作的一部分，我们计划研究使用可变大小 partition 可能带来的改进。

sections.title: 2.3 Operations
sections.originalMarkdown:
Ambry has a lightweight API supporting only 3 operations: put, get, and delete. The request handling procedure is shown in Figure 3. On receiving a request, the Frontend optionally conducts some security checks on the request. Then, using the Router Library (that contains the core logic of operation handling) it chooses a partition, communicates with the Datanode(s) in charge, and serves the request. In the put operation, the partition is chosen randomly (for data balancing purposes), and in the get/delete operation the partition is extracted from the blob id.

Operations are handled in a multi-master design where operations can be served by any of the replicas. The decision of how many replicas to contact is based on user-defined policies. These policies are similar to consistency levels in Cassandra [14], where they control how many (one, k, majority, all) replicas to involve in an operation. For puts (or deletes), the request is forwarded to all replicas, and policies define the number of acknowledgments needed for a success (trade-off between durability and latency). For gets, policies determine how many randomly selected replicas to contact for the operation (trade-off between resources usage and latency). In practice, we found that for all operations the k = 2 replica policy gives us the balance we desire. Stricter polices (involving more replicas) can be used to provide stronger consistency guarantees.

Additionally, performing write operations to all replicas placed in multiple geo-distributed datacenters in a synchronous fashion can affect the latency and throughput. In order to alleviate this issue, Ambry uses asynchronous writes where puts are performed synchronously only in the local datacenter, i.e., the datacenter in which the Frontend receiving the request is located. The request is counted as successfully finished at this point. Later on, the blob is replicated to other datacenters using a lightweight replication algorithm (Section 5).

In order to provide read-after-write consistency in a datacenter which a blob has not been replicated yet (e.g., writing to one datacenter and reading from another), Ambry uses proxy requests. If the Frontend cannot retrieve a blob from its local datacenter, it proxies the request to another datacenter and returns the result from there. Although a proxy request is expensive, in practice we found that proxy requests happen infrequently (less than 0.001 % of the time).
sections.translationMarkdown:
Ambry 提供一个轻量级 API，仅支持三种操作：put、get 和 delete。图 3 展示了请求处理过程。Frontend 收到请求后，可以选择对请求执行一些安全检查。随后，它使用包含操作处理核心逻辑的 Router Library 选择一个 partition，与负责该 partition 的 Datanode 通信并服务请求。在 put 操作中，系统出于数据均衡目的随机选择 partition；在 get/delete 操作中，则从 blob id 提取 partition。

系统采用 multi-master 设计处理操作，任意副本都可以服务操作。要联系多少个副本由用户定义的策略决定。这些策略类似于 Cassandra [14] 的 consistency level，用于控制一次操作涉及多少个副本（one、k、majority 或 all）。对于 put（或 delete），请求会被转发给所有副本，策略定义成功所需的确认数量，即在持久性与延迟之间权衡。对于 get，策略决定该操作需要联系多少个随机选出的副本，即在资源使用与延迟之间权衡。在实践中，我们发现对于所有操作，k = 2 副本策略能够实现所需的平衡。也可以使用涉及更多副本的更严格策略来提供更强的一致性保证。

此外，如果以同步方式向位于多个地理分布数据中心的所有副本执行写操作，延迟和吞吐量都会受到影响。为缓解这一问题，Ambry 使用异步写入：put 只在本地数据中心同步执行，即只在接收请求的 Frontend 所在的数据中心执行。到此时，该请求就被视为成功完成。随后，系统使用轻量级复制算法将 blob 复制到其他数据中心（第 5 节）。

为了在 blob 尚未复制到的数据中心提供 read-after-write consistency（例如写入一个数据中心、从另一个数据中心读取），Ambry 使用 proxy request。如果 Frontend 无法从本地数据中心检索 blob，它会将请求代理到另一个数据中心，并返回从那里获得的结果。尽管 proxy request 开销较高，但我们在实践中发现它很少发生（低于 0.001%）。

sections.title: 3. LOAD BALANCING
sections.originalMarkdown:
Skewed workloads, massively large blobs, and cluster expansions create load imbalance and impact the throughput and latency of the system. Ambry achieves load balancing (in terms of disk usage and request rates) in both static and dynamic (scale-out) clusters.

**Static Cluster:** Splitting large blobs into multiple small chunks (Section 4.2.1) as well as routing put operations to random partitions, achieves balance in partition sizes. Additionally, using fairly large partition sizes along with relying on CDNs to handle very popular data significantly decrease the likelihood of hot partitions. Using these techniques the load imbalance of request rates and partition sizes in production gets to as low as 5% amongst Datanodes.

**Dynamic Cluster:** In practice, read-write partitions receive all the write traffic and also the majority of the read traffic (due to popularity). Since partitions grow in a semi-balanced manner, the number of read-write partitions becomes the main factor of load imbalance. After cluster expansion, new Datanodes contain only read-write partitions, while older Datanodes contain mostly read-only partitions. This skewed distribution of read-write partitions creates a large imbalance in the system. In our initial version, the average request rates of new Datanodes were up to 100x higher than old Datanodes and 10x higher than the average-aged ones.

To alleviate this issue, Ambry employs a rebalancing mechanism that returns the cluster to a semi-balanced state (in terms of disk usage and request rate) with minimal data movement. The rebalancing approach reduces request rate and disk usage imbalance by 6-10x and 9-10x respectively.

Ambry defines the ideal (load balanced) state as a triplet `(idealRW, idealRO, idealUsed)` representing the ideal number of read-write partitions, ideal number of read-only partitions and ideal disk usage each disk should have. This ideal state `(idealRW, idealRO, idealUsed)` is computed by dividing the total number of read-write/read-only partitions and total used disk space by the number of disks in the cluster, respectively. A disk is considered above (or below) ideal if it has more (or less) read-write/read-only partitions or disk usage than the ideal state.

The rebalancing algorithm attempts to reach this ideal state. This is done by moving partitions from disks above ideal to disks below ideal using a 2 phase approach, as shown in the pseudo-code below.

**Algorithm 1 Rebalancing Algorithm**

```text
1:  // Compute ideal state.
2:  idealRW=totalNumRW / numDisks
3:  idealRO=totalNumRO / numDisks
4:  idealUsed=totalUsed / numDisks
5:  // Phase1: move extra partitions into a partition pool.
6:  partitionPool = {}
7:  for each disk d do
8:      // Move extra read-write partitions.
9:      while d.NumRW > idealRW do
10:         partitionPool += chooseMinimumUsedRW(d)
11:     // Move extra read-only partitions.
12:     while d.NumRO > idealRO & d.used > idealUsed do
13:         partitionPool += chooseRandomRO(d)
14: // Phase2: Move partitions to disks needing partitions.
15: placePartitions(read-write)
16: placePartitions(read-only)
17: function placePartitions(Type t)
18:     while partitionPool contains partitions type t do
19:         D=shuffleDisksBelowIdeal()
20:         for disk d in D and partition p in pool do
21:             d.addPartition(p)
22:             partitionPool.remove(p)
```

**Phase1 - Move to Partition Pool:** In this phase, Ambry moves partitions from disks above ideal into a pool, called `partitionPool` (Lines 6-13). At the end of this phase no disk should remain above ideal, unless removing any partition would cause it to fall below ideal.

Ambry starts from read-write partitions (which are the main factor), and moves extra ones solely based on `idealRW` threshold. The same process is repeated for read-only partitions, but with considering both `idealRO` and `idealUsed` when moving partitions. The strategy of choosing which partition to move is based on minimizing data movement. For read-write partitions, the one with the minimum used capacity is chosen, while for read-only partitions, a random one is chosen since all such partitions are full.

**Phase2 - Place Partitions on Disks:** In this phase, Ambry places partitions from the partition pool on disks below ideal (Lines 14-16), starting from read-write partitions and then read-only ones. Partitions are placed using a random round-robin approach (Line 17-22). Ambry finds all disks below ideal, shuffles them, and assigns partitions to them in a round-robin fashion. This procedure is repeated until the pool becomes empty.

After finding the the new placement, replicas are seamlessly moved by: 1) creating a new replica in the destination, 2) syncing the new replica with old ones using the replication protocol while serving new writes in all replicas, and 3) deleting the old replica after syncing.
sections.translationMarkdown:
倾斜的工作负载、超大型 blob 和集群扩容会造成负载不均衡，并影响系统吞吐量和延迟。无论在静态集群还是动态（横向扩展）集群中，Ambry 都能在磁盘使用量和请求率方面实现负载均衡。

**静态集群：** 将大型 blob 拆分为多个小 chunk（第 4.2.1 节），并将 put 操作路由到随机 partition，可以均衡 partition 大小。此外，采用较大的 partition，并依赖 CDN 处理极热门的数据，可显著降低出现热点 partition 的可能性。借助这些技术，生产环境中 Datanode 之间的请求率和 partition 大小不均衡程度最低可降至 5%。

**动态集群：** 在实践中，可读写 partition 承担全部写流量，并且由于数据热度的影响，还承担大部分读流量。由于各 partition 以近似均衡的方式增长，可读写 partition 的数量成为负载不均衡的主要因素。集群扩容后，新 Datanode 仅包含可读写 partition，而较老的 Datanode 主要包含只读 partition。可读写 partition 的这种倾斜分布会造成严重的系统不均衡。在最初版本中，新 Datanode 的平均请求率最高可达老 Datanode 的 100 倍、服役时间居中的 Datanode 的 10 倍。

为缓解这一问题，Ambry 使用重平衡机制，以最少的数据移动使集群在磁盘使用量和请求率方面恢复到近似均衡状态。该重平衡方法分别将请求率不均衡和磁盘使用量不均衡降低 6–10 倍和 9–10 倍。

Ambry 将理想的负载均衡状态定义为三元组 `(idealRW, idealRO, idealUsed)`，分别表示每块磁盘应具有的理想可读写 partition 数、理想只读 partition 数和理想磁盘使用量。理想状态 `(idealRW, idealRO, idealUsed)` 的计算方法是：分别用可读写/只读 partition 总数以及已使用磁盘空间总量，除以集群中的磁盘数量。如果一块磁盘的可读写/只读 partition 数或磁盘使用量高于（或低于）理想状态，则认为该磁盘高于（或低于）理想状态。

重平衡算法试图达到这一理想状态。如以下伪代码所示，它采用两阶段方法，将 partition 从高于理想状态的磁盘移动到低于理想状态的磁盘。

**算法 1 重平衡算法**

```text
1:  // 计算理想状态。
2:  idealRW=totalNumRW / numDisks
3:  idealRO=totalNumRO / numDisks
4:  idealUsed=totalUsed / numDisks
5:  // 阶段 1：将多余 partition 移入 partition pool。
6:  partitionPool = {}
7:  for each disk d do
8:      // 移动多余的可读写 partition。
9:      while d.NumRW > idealRW do
10:         partitionPool += chooseMinimumUsedRW(d)
11:     // 移动多余的只读 partition。
12:     while d.NumRO > idealRO & d.used > idealUsed do
13:         partitionPool += chooseRandomRO(d)
14: // 阶段 2：将 partition 移至需要 partition 的磁盘。
15: placePartitions(read-write)
16: placePartitions(read-only)
17: function placePartitions(Type t)
18:     while partitionPool contains partitions type t do
19:         D=shuffleDisksBelowIdeal()
20:         for disk d in D and partition p in pool do
21:             d.addPartition(p)
22:             partitionPool.remove(p)
```

**阶段 1——移入 Partition Pool：** 在此阶段，Ambry 将 partition 从高于理想状态的磁盘移入名为 `partitionPool` 的池中（第 6–13 行）。阶段结束时，除非移除任何一个 partition 都会使磁盘低于理想状态，否则不应再有磁盘高于理想状态。

Ambry 首先处理可读写 partition（这是主要因素），并且仅依据 `idealRW` 阈值移动多余的 partition。随后对只读 partition 重复相同过程，但移动时同时考虑 `idealRO` 和 `idealUsed`。选择要移动哪个 partition 的策略以尽量减少数据移动为目标。对于可读写 partition，选择已用容量最少的一个；对于只读 partition，由于它们全都已满，因此随机选择一个。

**阶段 2——在磁盘上放置 Partition：** 在此阶段，Ambry 将 partition pool 中的 partition 放置到低于理想状态的磁盘上（第 14–16 行），先放置可读写 partition，再放置只读 partition。partition 采用随机 round-robin 方法放置（第 17–22 行）。Ambry 找出所有低于理想状态的磁盘，将其顺序打乱，然后以 round-robin 方式为其分配 partition。重复此过程，直到 pool 为空。

确定新放置方案后，系统通过以下步骤无缝移动副本：1）在目标位置创建新副本；2）使用复制协议将新副本与旧副本同步，同时所有副本继续服务新写入；3）同步完成后删除旧副本。

sections.title: 4. COMPONENTS IN DETAIL
sections.originalMarkdown: In this section we further discuss the main components of Ambry. We describe the detailed state stored by the Cluster Manager (Section 4.1), extra responsibilities of Frontends including chunking and failure detection (Section 4.2), and additional structures maintained by the Datanodes (Section 4.3).
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节进一步讨论 Ambry 的主要组件。我们将介绍 Cluster Manager 存储的详细状态（第 4.1 节）、Frontend 承担的额外职责，包括 chunking 和故障检测（第 4.2 节），以及 Datanode 维护的额外结构（第 4.3 节）。

sections.title: 4.1 Cluster Manager
sections.originalMarkdown: The Cluster Manager is in charge of maintaining the state of the cluster. Each datacenter has its local Cluster Manager instance(s) kept in-sync with others using Zookeeper. The state stored by the Cluster Manager is very small (less than a few MBs in total), consisting of a hardware and logical layout.
sections.translationMarkdown: Cluster Manager 负责维护集群状态。每个数据中心都有本地 Cluster Manager 实例，并使用 Zookeeper 与其他实例保持同步。Cluster Manager 存储的状态非常小（总共不到几 MB），由硬件布局和逻辑布局组成。

sections.title: 4.1.1 Hardware Layout
sections.originalMarkdown:
The hardware layout includes information about the physical structure of the cluster, i.e., the arrangement of datacenters, Datanodes, and disks. It also maintains the raw capacity and status, i.e., healthy (UP) or failed (DOWN), for each disk. An example hardware layout is shown in Table 1. As shown, Ambry works in a heterogeneous environment with different hardware and configuration used inside and across different datacenters.

| Datacenter | Datanode | Disk | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---:|---|
| DC 1 | Datanode 1 | disk 1 | 4 TB | UP |
|  |  | ... | ... | ... |
|  |  | disk k | 4 TB | UP |
| DC 1 | Datanode 2 | disk 1 | 4 TB | DOWN |
|  |  | ... | ... | ... |
|  |  | disk k′ | 4 TB | UP |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
| DC n | Datanode j | disk 1 | 1 TB | DOWN |
|  |  | ... | ... | ... |
|  |  | disk k″ | 1 TB | UP |

*Table 1: Hardware layout in Cluster Manager.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
硬件布局包含集群物理结构的信息，即数据中心、Datanode 和磁盘的组织方式。它还维护每块磁盘的原始容量和状态，即健康（UP）或故障（DOWN）。表 1 展示了一个硬件布局示例。如表所示，Ambry 可在异构环境中运行，同一数据中心内部以及不同数据中心之间可以使用不同的硬件和配置。

| 数据中心 | Datanode | 磁盘 | 大小 | 状态 |
|---|---|---|---:|---|
| DC 1 | Datanode 1 | disk 1 | 4 TB | UP |
|  |  | ... | ... | ... |
|  |  | disk k | 4 TB | UP |
| DC 1 | Datanode 2 | disk 1 | 4 TB | DOWN |
|  |  | ... | ... | ... |
|  |  | disk k′ | 4 TB | UP |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
| DC n | Datanode j | disk 1 | 1 TB | DOWN |
|  |  | ... | ... | ... |
|  |  | disk k″ | 1 TB | UP |

*表 1：Cluster Manager 中的硬件布局。*

sections.title: 4.1.2 Logical Layout
sections.originalMarkdown:
The logical layout maintains the physical location of partition replicas, and the state (read-only/read-write) of each partition. In order to find the state of a partition, the Cluster Manager periodically contacts the Datanodes, and requests the state of their partitions. This layout is used for choosing a partition to write a new blob to (put operation), and locating the Datanode in charge of a given replica (all operations). An example of this layout is shown in Table 2. As shown, replicas of a partition can be placed on multiple Datanodes in one datacenter, and/or in different datacenters. Additionally, one disk (e.g., DC 1: Datanode 1: disk 1) can contain replicas of distinct partitions, where some are read-only and some are read-write. Partitions are added by updating the logical layout stored in the Cluster Manager instances[^2]. | Partition id | State | Placement | |---|---|---| | partition 1 | read-write | DC 1: Datanode 1: disk 1
DC 1: Datanode 4: disk 5
...
DC 3: Datanode 7: disk 2 | | ... | ... | ... | | partition p | read-only | DC 1: Datanode 1: disk 1
...
DC 4: Datanode 5: disk 2 | *Table 2: Logical Layout in Cluster Manager.* [^2]: Currently, the system administrator manually adds partitions in order to prevent unwanted and rapid cluster growths. However, this can easily be automated.
sections.translationMarkdown:
逻辑布局维护 partition 副本的物理位置，以及每个 partition 的状态（只读/可读写）。为了确定 partition 的状态，Cluster Manager 会定期联系 Datanode，并请求其 partition 状态。该布局用于选择写入新 blob 的 partition（put 操作），以及定位负责给定副本的 Datanode（所有操作）。表 2 展示了一个逻辑布局示例。如表所示，一个 partition 的副本可以放置在同一数据中心的多个 Datanode 上，也可以放置在不同数据中心中。此外，一块磁盘（例如 DC 1: Datanode 1: disk 1）可以包含不同 partition 的副本，其中一些为只读，一些为可读写。添加 partition 时，需要更新 Cluster Manager 实例所存储的逻辑布局[^2]。 | Partition id | 状态 | 放置位置 | |---|---|---| | partition 1 | read-write | DC 1: Datanode 1: disk 1
DC 1: Datanode 4: disk 5
...
DC 3: Datanode 7: disk 2 | | ... | ... | ... | | partition p | read-only | DC 1: Datanode 1: disk 1
...
DC 4: Datanode 5: disk 2 | *表 2：Cluster Manager 中的逻辑布局。* [^2]: 当前，系统管理员会手动添加 partition，以防止集群出现意外的快速增长。不过，这一过程很容易实现自动化。

sections.title: 4.2 Frontend Layer
sections.originalMarkdown:
The Frontend is the entry point to Ambry for external requests. Each datacenter has its own set of Frontends. Frontends are decentralized involving no master or coordination, identically performing the same task, and stateless with all state stored in the Cluster Manager (which is periodically pulled). This design enhances scalability (new Frontends can be added without much performance penalty), fault-tolerance (requests can be forwarded to any Frontend), and failure recovery (failed Frontends can quickly be replaced) for Frontends. Frontends have three main responsibilities:

1. **Request Handling:** This involves receiving requests, routing them to the corresponding Datanode(s) using the Router Library (Section 4.2.1), and sending back the response.
2. **Security Checks:** Optionally performing security checks, such as virus scanning and authentication on requests.
3. **Capturing Operations:** Pushing events to a change capture system out of Ambry for further offline analysis, such as finding request patterns of the system. We use Kafka [13] as our change-capture system due to the high durability, high throughput, and low overhead it provides.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Frontend 是外部请求进入 Ambry 的入口。每个数据中心都有自己的一组 Frontend。Frontend 采用去中心化设计，不涉及 master 或协调；它们执行完全相同的任务，并且无状态，所有状态都存储在 Cluster Manager 中并由 Frontend 定期拉取。该设计增强了 Frontend 的可扩展性（可以添加新的 Frontend，而不会造成太大性能损失）、容错能力（请求可以转发到任意 Frontend）和故障恢复能力（发生故障的 Frontend 可以迅速替换）。Frontend 有三项主要职责：

1. **请求处理：** 接收请求，使用 Router Library（第 4.2.1 节）将请求路由到相应的 Datanode，并返回响应。
2. **安全检查：** 可选择对请求执行安全检查，例如病毒扫描和身份认证。
3. **操作捕获：** 将事件推送到 Ambry 之外的 change capture 系统，以便进行进一步的离线分析，例如识别系统的请求模式。我们使用 Kafka [13] 作为 change-capture 系统，因为它能够提供高持久性、高吞吐量和低开销。

sections.title: 4.2.1 Router Library
sections.originalMarkdown:
The Router Library contains all the core logic of handling requests and communicating with Datanodes. Frontends simply embed and use this library. Clients can bypass Frontends by embedding this library and directly serving requests. This library includes four main procedures: 1) policy-based routing, 2) chunking large blobs, 3) failure detection, and 4) proxy requests.

**Policy Based Routing:** On receiving a request, the library decides which partition to involve (randomly chosen for puts and extracted from blob id for gets/deletes). Then, based on the policy used (`{one, k, majority, all}` discussed in Section 2.3), it communicates with the corresponding replica(s) until the request is served/failed.

**Chunking:** Extremely large blobs (e.g., videos) create load imbalance, block smaller blobs, and inherently have high latency. To mitigate these issues, Ambry splits large blobs into smaller equal-size units called chunks. A large chunk size does not fully resolve the large blob challenges and a small chunk size adds too much overhead. Based on our current large blob size distribution, we found the sweet spot for the chunk size to be in the range of 4 to 8 MB[^3].

During a put operation, a blob *b* is split into *k* chunks `{c₁, c₂, ..., cₖ}`, each treated as an independent blob. Each chunk goes through the same steps as a normal put blob operation (Section 2.3), most likely being placed on a different partition. It is also assigned a unique chunk id with the same format as a blob id. In order to be able to retrieve *b* Ambry creates a metadata blob *bmetadata* for *b*. *bmetadata* stores the number of chunks and chunk ids in order, as shown in Figure 4. This metadata blob is then put into Ambry as a normal blob and the blob id of *bmetadata* is returned to the user as the blob id of *b*. If the put fails before writing all chunks, the system will issue deletes for written chunks and the operation has to be redone.

During a get, the metadata blob is retrieved and chunk ids are extracted from it. Then, Ambry uses a sliding buffer of size *s* to retrieve the blob. Ambry queries the first *s* chunks of the blob independently and in parallel (since they are most likely written on unique partitions placed on separate Datanodes). When the first chunk in the buffer is retrieved, Ambry slides the buffer to the next chunk, and so on. The whole blob starts being returned to the user the moment the first chunk of the blob is retrieved.

Although an extra put/get is needed in this chunking mechanism (for the metadata blob), overall, our approach improves latency since multiple chunks are written and retrieved in parallel.

**Zero-cost Failure Detection:** Failures happen frequently in a large system. They range from unresponsiveness and connection timeouts, to disk I/O problems. Thus, Ambry needs a failure detection mechanism to discover unavailable Datanodes/disks and avoid forwarding requests to them.

Ambry employs a zero-cost failure detection mechanism involving no extra messages, such as heartbeats and pings, by leveraging request messages. In practice, we found our failure detection mechanism is effective, simple, and consumes very little bandwidth. This mechanism is shown in Figure 5. In this approach, Ambry keeps track of the number of consecutive failed requests for a particular Datanode (or disk) in the last check period of time. If this number exceeds a `MAX_FAIL` threshold (in our example set to 2) the Datanode is marked as temporarily down for a wait period of time. In this state all queued requests for this Datanode will eventually time out and need to be reattempted by the user. After the wait period has passed, the Datanode becomes temporarily available. When a Datanode is in the temporarily available phase, if the next request sent to that Datanode fails, it will move to the temporarily down phase again. Otherwise, it will be marked as available, working as normal again.

**Proxy Requests:** As described in Section 2.3, Ambry uses proxy requests to reach higher availability and read-after-write consistency in remote datacenters. When a blob has not been replicated in the local datacenter yet, requests for that blob are forwarded to other datacenters and served there (proxy requests). However, datacenter partitions can cause unavailability of unreplicated data until the partition is healed and replicas converge.

Proxy requests are handled by the Router Library, transparently from the user issuing the request. In practice, we found proxy requests occur less than 0.001% of the time, thus minimally affecting the user experience.

[^3]: Chunk size is not fixed and can be adapted to follow the growth in blob sizes, improvements in network, etc.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Router Library 包含处理请求以及与 Datanode 通信的全部核心逻辑。Frontend 只需嵌入并使用该 library。客户端也可以嵌入该 library，绕过 Frontend 直接服务请求。Router Library 包括四个主要过程：1）基于策略的路由；2）对大型 blob 进行 chunking；3）故障检测；4）proxy request。

**基于策略的路由：** 收到请求后，library 决定涉及哪个 partition（put 随机选择，get/delete 从 blob id 中提取）。随后，它依据所使用的策略（第 2.3 节讨论的 `{one, k, majority, all}`）与相应副本通信，直到请求得到服务或失败。

**Chunking：** 超大型 blob（例如视频）会造成负载不均衡、阻塞较小的 blob，并且天然具有较高延迟。为缓解这些问题，Ambry 将大型 blob 拆分成称为 chunk、大小相等的较小单元。chunk 过大无法完全解决大型 blob 带来的挑战，chunk 过小则会引入过多开销。根据目前大型 blob 的大小分布，我们发现 chunk 大小的最佳区间为 4–8 MB[^3]。

在 put 操作期间，blob *b* 被拆分为 *k* 个 chunk `{c₁, c₂, ..., cₖ}`，每个 chunk 都被视为独立 blob。每个 chunk 都执行与普通 blob put 操作相同的步骤（第 2.3 节），并且很可能被放置在不同的 partition 上。系统还会为其分配唯一的 chunk id，其格式与 blob id 相同。为了能够检索 *b*，Ambry 为 *b* 创建一个 metadata blob *bmetadata*。如图 4 所示，*bmetadata* 按顺序存储 chunk 的数量及其 id。随后，这个 metadata blob 会作为普通 blob put 到 Ambry 中，而 *bmetadata* 的 blob id 会作为 *b* 的 blob id 返回给用户。如果在所有 chunk 写完前 put 失败，系统会对已写入的 chunk 发出 delete，之后必须重新执行该操作。

执行 get 时，系统首先检索 metadata blob，并从中提取 chunk id。然后，Ambry 使用大小为 *s* 的滑动 buffer 检索 blob。Ambry 独立且并行地查询 blob 的前 *s* 个 chunk，因为它们很可能被写入放置在不同 Datanode 上的不同 partition。当 buffer 中的第一个 chunk 被检索到后，Ambry 将 buffer 滑动到下一个 chunk，并以此类推。一旦 blob 的第一个 chunk 被检索到，系统就开始向用户返回整个 blob。

尽管该 chunking 机制需要为 metadata blob 额外执行一次 put/get，但总体而言，由于多个 chunk 可以并行写入和检索，我们的方法改善了延迟。

**零成本故障检测：** 大型系统中经常发生故障，范围从无响应、连接超时到磁盘 I/O 问题。因此，Ambry 需要故障检测机制来发现不可用的 Datanode/磁盘，并避免将请求转发给它们。

Ambry 利用请求消息实现零成本故障检测，不需要 heartbeat、ping 等任何额外消息。实践表明，该故障检测机制有效、简单，并且仅消耗极少带宽。图 5 展示了这一机制。Ambry 会记录在最近一个检查时间段内，某个 Datanode（或磁盘）的连续失败请求数。如果该数量超过 `MAX_FAIL` 阈值（示例中设为 2），Datanode 会在一段等待时间内被标记为暂时宕机。在此状态下，所有面向该 Datanode 的排队请求最终都会超时，需要用户重新尝试。等待时间结束后，Datanode 变为暂时可用。当 Datanode 处于暂时可用阶段时，如果发送给它的下一个请求失败，它会再次进入暂时宕机阶段；否则会被标记为可用，并恢复正常工作。

**Proxy Request：** 如第 2.3 节所述，Ambry 使用 proxy request 在远程数据中心实现更高的可用性和 read-after-write consistency。当 blob 尚未复制到本地数据中心时，对该 blob 的请求会被转发到其他数据中心并由其服务。不过，数据中心之间的网络分区可能导致尚未复制的数据不可用，直到分区恢复且副本收敛。

Proxy request 由 Router Library 处理，对发出请求的用户透明。实践中，proxy request 的发生比例低于 0.001%，因此对用户体验的影响极小。

[^3]: Chunk 大小并不固定，可以根据 blob 大小增长、网络改进等情况进行调整。

sections.title: 4.3 Datanode Layer
sections.originalMarkdown:
Datanodes are in charge of maintaining the actual data. Each Datanode manages a number of disks, and responds to requests for partition replicas placed on its disks. Puts are handled by writing to the end of the partition file. Gets can be more time-consuming, especially since the location of the blob in the partition is not known. To minimize both read and write latencies, Datanodes employ a few techniques:

- **Indexing blobs:** Ambry stores an index of blob offsets per partition replica to prevent sequential sweeps for finding blobs (Section 4.3.1).
- **Exploiting OS cache:** Ambry utilizes OS caching to serve most reads from the RAM, by limiting the RAM usage of other components (Section 4.3.2).
- **Batched writes, with a single disk seek:** Ambry batches writes for a particular partition together and periodically flushes the writes to disk. Thus, it incurs at most one disk seek for a batch of sequential writes. The flush period is configurable and trades off latency for durability. Although, batching can introduce overheads of flushing, dirty buffers, and tuning, the benefits outweigh these overheads.
- **Keeping all file handles open:** Since partitions are typically very large (100 GB in our setting), the number of partition replicas placed on a Datanode is small (a few hundred). Thus, Ambry keeps all file handles open at all times.
- **Zero copy gets:** When reading a blob, Ambry utilizes a zero copy [25] mechanism, i.e., the kernel directly copies data from disk to the network buffer without going through the application. This is feasible since the Datanodes do not perform any computation on the data at get operations.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Datanode 负责维护实际数据。每个 Datanode 管理若干磁盘，并响应针对其磁盘上 partition 副本的请求。put 通过写入 partition 文件末尾来处理。get 可能更耗时，尤其是因为 blob 在 partition 中的位置未知。为了尽量降低读写延迟，Datanode 采用了以下技术：

- **为 blob 建立索引：** Ambry 为每个 partition 副本存储 blob 偏移索引，避免为了查找 blob 而执行顺序扫描（第 4.3.1 节）。
- **利用操作系统缓存：** Ambry 通过限制其他组件的 RAM 使用量，利用操作系统缓存从 RAM 服务大多数读请求（第 4.3.2 节）。
- **批量写入且只执行一次磁盘寻道：** Ambry 将针对某个 partition 的写入批量聚合，并定期将它们刷入磁盘。因此，一批顺序写入最多只引发一次磁盘寻道。刷盘周期可以配置，用于在延迟与持久性之间进行权衡。尽管 batching 可能引入刷盘、dirty buffer 和调优方面的开销，但其收益超过了这些开销。
- **保持所有文件句柄打开：** 由于 partition 通常非常大（在我们的配置中为 100 GB），一个 Datanode 上放置的 partition 副本数量较少（几百个）。因此，Ambry 始终保持所有文件句柄打开。
- **Zero copy get：** 读取 blob 时，Ambry 使用 zero copy [25] 机制，即内核不经过应用程序，直接将数据从磁盘复制到网络 buffer。之所以可行，是因为 Datanode 在 get 操作期间不对数据执行任何计算。

sections.title: 4.3.1 Indexing
sections.originalMarkdown:
To find the location of a blob in a partition replica with low latency, the Datanode maintains a light-weight in-memory indexing per replica, as shown in Figure 6. The indexing is sorted by blob id, mapping the blob id to the start offset of the blob entry. The indexing is updated in an online fashion whenever blobs are put (e.g., blob 60) or deleted (e.g., blob 20).

Similar to SSTables [5], Ambry limits the size of the index by splitting it into segments, storing old segments on disk, and maintaining a Bloom filter for each on-disk segment (Section 4.3.2).

The indexing also stores a flag indicating if a blob has been deleted and optionally a time-to-live (TTL). During get operations, if the blob has expired or been deleted, an error will be returned before reading the actual data.

Note that the indexing does not contain any additional information affecting the correctness of the system, and just improves performance. If a Datanode fails, the whole indexing can be reconstructed from the partition.
sections.translationMarkdown:
为了以低延迟找到 blob 在 partition 副本中的位置，如图 6 所示，Datanode 为每个副本维护一个轻量级内存索引。该索引按 blob id 排序，将 blob id 映射到 blob entry 的起始偏移。每当 blob 被 put（例如 blob 60）或 delete（例如 blob 20）时，索引都会在线更新。

与 SSTable [5] 类似，Ambry 通过将索引划分为 segment 来限制其大小：旧 segment 存储在磁盘上，并为每个磁盘 segment 维护一个 Bloom filter（第 4.3.2 节）。

索引还存储一个标志来指示 blob 是否已删除，并可选择存储 time-to-live（TTL）。执行 get 时，如果 blob 已过期或已被删除，系统会在读取实际数据之前返回错误。

需要注意，索引不包含任何影响系统正确性的额外信息，只用于改善性能。如果 Datanode 发生故障，可以根据 partition 重建完整索引。

sections.title: 4.3.2 Exploiting The OS Cache
sections.originalMarkdown:
Recently written data, which is the popular data as well, is automatically cached without any extra overhead (by the operating system). By exploiting this feature, many reads can be served from memory, which significantly improves performance. Thus, Ambry limits the memory usage of other data structures in the Datanode. Ambry bounds the indexing by splitting it into segments, with only the latest segment remaining in-memory (Figure 6). New entries are added to the in-memory segment of the indexing. When the in-memory segment exceeds a maximum size it is flushed to disk as a read-only segment. This design also helps toward failure recovery since only the in-memory segment needs to be reconstructed. Looking up blob offsets is done in reverse chronological order, starting with the latest segment (in-memory segment). Thus, a delete entry will be found before a put entry when reading a blob. This ensures deleted blobs are not retrievable.

**Bloom Filters:** To reduce lookup latency for on-disk segments, Ambry maintains an in-memory Bloom filter for each segment, containing the blob ids in that index segment. Using Bloom filters, Ambry quickly filters out which on-disk segment to load. Thus, with high probability, it incurs only one disk seek. However, due to our skewed workload a majority of reads just hit the in-memory segment, without any disk seeks.
sections.translationMarkdown:
最近写入的数据通常也是热门数据，操作系统会自动缓存这些数据，不产生任何额外开销。利用这一特性，许多读请求都可以从内存中获得服务，从而显著改善性能。因此，Ambry 会限制 Datanode 中其他数据结构的内存使用量。Ambry 将索引划分为多个 segment 来约束其大小，并只将最新 segment 保留在内存中（图 6）。新 entry 被添加到索引的内存 segment。当内存 segment 超过最大大小时，它会作为只读 segment 刷入磁盘。这一设计也有助于故障恢复，因为只需重建内存 segment。查找 blob 偏移时按时间倒序进行，从最新 segment（内存 segment）开始。因此，在读取 blob 时，delete entry 会先于 put entry 被找到，从而确保已删除的 blob 无法被检索。

**Bloom Filter：** 为降低磁盘 segment 的查找延迟，Ambry 为每个 segment 维护一个内存 Bloom filter，其中包含该索引 segment 内的 blob id。借助 Bloom filter，Ambry 可以快速筛选出需要加载的磁盘 segment。因此，它很可能只需要一次磁盘寻道。不过，由于我们的工作负载存在倾斜，大多数读请求只会命中内存 segment，完全不需要磁盘寻道。

sections.title: 5. REPLICATION
sections.originalMarkdown:
Replicas belonging to the same partition can become out of sync due to either failures, or asynchronous writes that write to only one datacenter. In order to fix inconsistent replicas, Ambry uses an asynchronous replication algorithm that periodically synchronizes replicas. This algorithm is completely decentralized. In this procedure each replica individually acts as a master and syncs-up with other replicas, in an all-to-all fashion. Synchronization is done using an asynchronous two-phase replication protocol as follows. This protocol is a pull-based approach where each replica independently requests for missing blobs from other replicas.

- **First Phase:** This phase finds missing blobs since the last synchronization point. This is done by requesting blob ids of all blobs written since the latest syncing offset and then filtering the ones missing locally.
- **Second Phase:** This phase replicates missing blobs. A request for only the missing blobs is sent. Then, the missing blobs are transferred and appended to the replica.

In order to find the recently written blobs quickly, the replication algorithm maintains an additional data structure per replica, called a journal. The journal is an in-memory cache of recent blobs ordered by their offset. Figure 7 shows example journals of two replicas (r1 and r2) and the two phase replication procedure for r1 syncing with r2 from latestOffset 600. In the first phase, r1 requests all recently added blobs in r2 after latestOffset; using the journal r2 returns a list B ={55, 40, 70, 90} of blob ids; and r1 filters out the missing blobs (blob 55 and 90). In the second phase, r1 receives the missing blobs, appends the blobs to the end of the replica, and updates the journal, indexing and latestOffset.

*Figure 7: Journals for two replicas of the same partition and an example of the replication algorithm.*

To provide improved efficiency and scalability of the system, the replication algorithm employs a number of further optimizations:

- Using separate thread pools for inter- and intra-datacenter replication with different periods.
- Batching requests between common partition replicas of two Datanodes, and batching blobs transferred across datacenters.
- Prioritizing lagging replicas to catch up at a faster rate (by using dedicated threads for lagging replicas).
sections.translationMarkdown:
属于同一 partition 的副本可能会由于故障，或仅写入一个 datacenter 的异步写入而失去同步。为了修复不一致的副本，Ambry 使用一种定期同步副本的异步复制算法。该算法是完全去中心化的。在此过程中，每个副本都独立充当 master，并以全互联方式与其他副本同步。同步通过如下异步两阶段复制协议完成。该协议采用 pull-based 方法，每个副本独立地向其他副本请求缺失的 blob。

- **第一阶段：** 此阶段查找自上次同步点以来缺失的 blob。具体做法是请求自最新同步 offset 以来写入的所有 blob id，然后过滤出本地缺失的 blob。
- **第二阶段：** 此阶段复制缺失的 blob。系统只发送针对缺失 blob 的请求，随后传输这些 blob 并将其追加到副本中。

为了快速找到最近写入的 blob，复制算法为每个副本维护一个称为 journal 的附加数据结构。journal 是一个按 offset 排序的近期 blob 内存缓存。图 7 展示了两个副本（r1 和 r2）的 journal 示例，以及 r1 从 latestOffset 600 开始与 r2 同步的两阶段复制过程。在第一阶段，r1 请求 r2 中 latestOffset 之后最近添加的所有 blob；r2 利用 journal 返回 blob id 列表 B ={55, 40, 70, 90}；r1 随后过滤出缺失的 blob（blob 55 和 90）。在第二阶段，r1 接收缺失的 blob，将其追加到副本末尾，并更新 journal、indexing 和 latestOffset。

*图 7：同一 partition 的两个副本的 journal，以及复制算法示例。*

为了进一步提高系统效率和可扩展性，复制算法采用了多项优化：

- 为 datacenter 间和 datacenter 内复制使用独立的线程池，并设置不同的执行周期。
- 对两个 Datanode 上共有 partition 副本之间的请求进行 batching，并对跨 datacenter 传输的 blob 进行 batching。
- 优先处理落后的副本，使其以更快速度赶上（为落后副本使用专用线程）。

sections.title: 6. EXPERIMENTAL RESULTS
sections.originalMarkdown: We perform three kinds of experiments: small cluster (Section 6.1), production cluster (Section 6.2), and simulations (Section 6.3).
sections.translationMarkdown: 我们进行了三类实验：小型集群实验（第 6.1 节）、生产集群实验（第 6.2 节）和模拟实验（第 6.3 节）。

sections.title: 6.1 Throughput and Latency
sections.originalMarkdown: In this section we measure the latency and throughput of the system using a micro-benchmark that stress-tests the system (Section 6.1.1), under read-only, write-only and read-write workloads.
sections.translationMarkdown: 本节使用一个对系统进行压力测试的 micro-benchmark（第 6.1.1 节），测量系统在只读、只写和读写混合 workload 下的延迟与吞吐量。

sections.title: 6.1.1 Micro-Benchmark
sections.originalMarkdown:
We first measure the peak throughput. We designed a micro-benchmark that linearly adds load to the system (by adding more clients), until the saturation point where no more requests can be handled. Each client sends requests one at a time with the next request sent right after a response.

This benchmark has three modes: Write, Read, and Read-Write. In Write mode, random byte array blobs are put with varying number of clients. In Read mode, first blobs are written at saturation rate for a write-period of time. Then, randomly chosen blobs are read from the written blobs[^4]. In most experiments we set the write-period long enough that most read requests (>80%) are served by disk, rather than RAM. Read-Write is a similar to Read, but serving 50% reads and 50% writes after the write-period.

Since latency and throughput are highly correlated with blob size, we use fixed-size blobs in each run, but vary the blob size.

[^4]: The random strategy gives a lower bound on the system’s performance since real-world access patterns are skewed toward recent data.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们首先测量峰值吞吐量。我们设计了一个 micro-benchmark，通过增加 client 数量向系统线性增加负载，直至系统无法再处理更多请求的饱和点。每个 client 每次发送一个请求，并在收到响应后立即发送下一个请求。

该 benchmark 有三种模式：Write、Read 和 Read-Write。在 Write 模式下，数量不同的 client 执行随机字节数组 blob 的 put。在 Read 模式下，首先以饱和速率写入 blob，持续一段 write-period；随后从已写入的 blob 中随机选择 blob 读取[^4]。在大多数实验中，我们将 write-period 设置得足够长，使绝大多数读请求（>80%）由磁盘而非 RAM 提供服务。Read-Write 与 Read 类似，但在 write-period 之后提供 50% 读和 50% 写。

由于延迟和吞吐量与 blob 大小高度相关，我们在每次运行中使用固定大小的 blob，但在不同运行之间改变 blob 大小。

[^4]: 随机策略给出了系统性能的下界，因为真实世界的访问模式会偏向近期数据。

sections.title: 6.1.2 Experimental Setup
sections.originalMarkdown:
We deployed Ambry with a single Datanode. The Datanode was running on a 24 core CPU with 64 GB of RAM, 14 1TB HDD disks, and a full-duplex 1 Gb/s Ethernet network. 4 GB of the RAM was set aside for the Datanode’s internal use and the rest was left to be used as Linux Cache. We created 8 single-replica 100 GB partitions on each disk, with a total of 122 partitions. Using 14 disks with a 1 Gb/s network might look like an overkill. However, disk seeks are the dominant latency factor for small blobs. Since a large portion of blobs are small (< 50 KB), we need the parallelism created by using multiple disks (more detail in Section 6.1.4). Note that Ambry is designed as a cost-effective system using cheap HDD disks.

Clients send requests from a few machines located in the same datacenter as the Datanode. These clients, that are acting as Frontends, directly send requests to the Datanode. The micro-benchmark discussed above was used with varying blob sizes {25 KB, 50 KB, 100 KB, 250 KB, 500 KB, 1 MB, 5 MB}. We did not go above 5 MB since blobs are chunked beyond that point.
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们部署了一个仅含单个 Datanode 的 Ambry。该 Datanode 运行在配有 24 核 CPU、64 GB RAM、14 块 1 TB HDD 以及全双工 1 Gb/s 以太网的机器上。RAM 中预留 4 GB 供 Datanode 内部使用，其余部分留作 Linux Cache。我们在每块磁盘上创建了 8 个单副本、100 GB 的 partition，共计 122 个 partition。在 1 Gb/s 网络下使用 14 块磁盘看起来可能有些过度。然而，对于小 blob，磁盘 seek 是延迟的主要因素。由于很大一部分 blob 较小（< 50 KB），我们需要通过多磁盘获得并行性（详见第 6.1.4 节）。需要注意的是，Ambry 被设计为使用廉价 HDD 的高性价比系统。

Client 从与 Datanode 位于同一 datacenter 的若干机器发送请求。这些充当 Frontend 的 client 直接向 Datanode 发送请求。上述 micro-benchmark 使用了不同的 blob 大小 {25 KB, 50 KB, 100 KB, 250 KB, 500 KB, 1 MB, 5 MB}。由于超过 5 MB 后 blob 会被切分成 chunk，因此我们没有测试更大的 blob。

sections.title: 6.1.3 Effect of Number of Clients
sections.originalMarkdown:
We ran the micro-benchmark with varying blob sizes, while linearly increasing the number of clients. For Read mode, the write-period was set such that 6 times the RAM size, was first written. Figure 8a shows the throughput in terms of MB/s served by the system. Adding clients proportionally increases the throughput until the saturation point. Saturation occurs at 75%-88% of the network bandwidth. The only exception is reading small blobs due to frequent disk seeks (discussed in Section 6.1.4). Saturation is reached quickly (usually ≤ 6 clients) since clients send requests as fast as possible in the benchmark.

Figure 8b shows the latency normalized by blob size (i.e., average latency divided by blob size). Latency stays almost constant before reaching saturation point, and then increases linearly beyond the throughput saturation point. The linear increase in latency after saturation indicates the system does not add additional overhead beyond request serving.

*Figure 8: Throughput and latency of read and write requests with varying number of clients on different blob sizes. These results were gathered on a single Datanode deployment.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们使用不同大小的 blob 运行 micro-benchmark，同时线性增加 client 数量。对于 Read 模式，write-period 被设置为先写入相当于 RAM 容量 6 倍的数据。图 8a 展示了系统以 MB/s 表示的吞吐量。增加 client 会使吞吐量按比例提升，直至达到饱和点。饱和时吞吐量为网络带宽的 75%–88%。唯一的例外是小 blob 的读取，这是由频繁的磁盘 seek 引起的（见第 6.1.4 节）。由于 benchmark 中的 client 以尽可能快的速度发送请求，系统很快就会饱和（通常 ≤ 6 个 client）。

图 8b 展示了按 blob 大小归一化后的延迟（即平均延迟除以 blob 大小）。达到饱和点之前，延迟几乎保持不变；超过吞吐量饱和点后，延迟线性增加。饱和后延迟的线性增长表明，除请求处理本身外，系统没有引入额外开销。

*图 8：不同 blob 大小下，随 client 数量变化的读写请求吞吐量和延迟。这些结果来自单 Datanode 部署。*

sections.title: 6.1.4 Effect of Blob Size
sections.originalMarkdown:
In Figures 9a and 9b we analyzed the maximum throughput (with 20 clients) under different blob sizes and workloads. For large objects (>200 KB), the maximum throughput (in MB/s) stays constant and close to maximum network bandwidth across all blob sizes. Similarly, throughput in terms of requests/s scales proportionally.

However, for Read and Read-Write, the read throughput in terms of MB/s drops linearly for smaller sizes. This drop is because our micro-benchmark reads blobs randomly, incurring frequent disk seeks. The effect of disk seeks is amplified for smaller blobs. By further profiling the disk using Bonnie++ [1] (an IO benchmark for measuring disk performance), we confirmed that disk seeks are the dominant source of latency for small blobs. For example, when reading a 50 KB blob, more than 94% of latency is due to disk seek (6.49 ms for disk seek, and 0.4 ms for reading the data).

Read and Write workload mostly utilize only the outbound and inbound link on the Datanode, respectively. However, Read-Write workload has a better mix and evenly utilizes both links reaching higher throughput. Therefore, in our full-duplex network infrastructure the Read-Write mode is able to saturate both links reaching almost double the network bandwidth (≃ 1.7 Gb/s in total out of the 2 Gb/s available). For smaller size objects it reaches twice the Read throughput, since Read-Write is a 50-50 workload with reads being the limiting factor.

Figure 9c demonstrates the trend in latency under various blob sizes. These results are before the saturation point with 2 clients. Similar to throughput, latency grows linearly except for reading small blobs. The higher latency in Read is because most read requests incur a disk seek, while write requests are batched and written together. The Read-Write latency falls halfway between Read and Write latency because of its mixed workload.

*Figure 9: Effect of blob size on maximum throughput, both in terms of MB/s and requests/s, and latency. Results were gathered on a write-only, read-only, and mixed (50%-50%) workload. Reads for small blob sizes (<200 KB) are slowed down by frequent disk seeks, while other requests saturate the network link.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
在图 9a 和图 9b 中，我们分析了不同 blob 大小和 workload 下的最大吞吐量（使用 20 个 client）。对于大对象（>200 KB），以 MB/s 表示的最大吞吐量在各种 blob 大小下均保持稳定，并接近最大网络带宽。类似地，以 requests/s 表示的吞吐量也按比例扩展。

然而，对于 Read 和 Read-Write，blob 较小时，以 MB/s 表示的读吞吐量会线性下降。这是因为我们的 micro-benchmark 随机读取 blob，从而产生频繁的磁盘 seek。blob 越小，磁盘 seek 的影响越明显。我们进一步使用 Bonnie++ [1]（用于测量磁盘性能的 IO benchmark）对磁盘进行 profiling，确认磁盘 seek 是小 blob 延迟的主要来源。例如，读取一个 50 KB blob 时，超过 94% 的延迟来自磁盘 seek（磁盘 seek 为 6.49 ms，读取数据为 0.4 ms）。

Read 和 Write workload 分别主要只使用 Datanode 的出站链路和入站链路。而 Read-Write workload 的请求组合更均衡，可以同时均匀使用两条链路，从而获得更高吞吐量。因此，在我们的全双工网络基础设施中，Read-Write 模式能够使两条链路都达到饱和，获得接近双倍网络带宽的吞吐量（可用总带宽 2 Gb/s，实际合计约为 1.7 Gb/s）。对于较小对象，其吞吐量可达到 Read 的两倍，因为 Read-Write 是 50-50 workload，而读是限制因素。

图 9c 展示了不同 blob 大小下的延迟趋势。这些结果是在使用 2 个 client、尚未达到饱和点时取得的。与吞吐量类似，除小 blob 读取外，延迟呈线性增长。Read 延迟更高，是因为大多数读请求会引发一次磁盘 seek，而写请求则会被 batching 后一起写入。由于采用混合 workload，Read-Write 延迟处于 Read 与 Write 延迟之间。

*图 9：blob 大小对最大吞吐量（分别以 MB/s 和 requests/s 表示）及延迟的影响。结果来自只写、只读和混合（50%-50%）workload。小 blob（<200 KB）的读取会因频繁磁盘 seek 而减慢，其他请求则能使网络链路饱和。*

sections.title: 6.1.5 Variance in Latency
sections.originalMarkdown:
The tail and variance in request latencies are important. Figure 10 shows the CDFs of Write, Read, and Read-Write mode experiments with 2 clients. The CDF of Read and Write mode is very close to a vertical line, with a short tail and a majority of values close to the median. The jump around 0.15 in Read mode (for 50 KB blob size) is because a small fraction of requests are served using the Linux cache which is orders of magnitudes faster than disk (Section 6.1.6). The Read-Write mode is a mixture of the Read and Write CDF with a change around 0.5, following the 50% read - 50% write workload pattern.

*Figure 10: CDF of read and write latency with 2 clients for various blob sizes. Read-Write falls in the middle with change around 0.5 due to the 50-50 mixed workload.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
请求延迟的尾部和方差十分重要。图 10 展示了使用 2 个 client 进行 Write、Read 和 Read-Write 模式实验时的 CDF。Read 和 Write 模式的 CDF 非常接近一条垂直线，尾部较短，且大多数值接近中位数。Read 模式（50 KB blob）在 0.15 附近的跳变，是因为少量请求由 Linux cache 提供服务，而 Linux cache 比磁盘快数个数量级（第 6.1.6 节）。Read-Write 模式是 Read 与 Write CDF 的混合，并在 0.5 附近发生变化，这与 50% 读、50% 写的 workload 模式相符。

*图 10：使用 2 个 client 时，不同 blob 大小下读写延迟的 CDF。由于采用 50-50 混合 workload，Read-Write 位于两者之间，并在 0.5 附近发生变化。*

sections.title: 6.1.6 Effect of Linux Cache
sections.originalMarkdown:
We ran the micro-benchmark on 50 KB blobs and 2 clients in two configurations: 1) writing 6 times more than the RAM size before reading, so that most requests (83 %) are served by disk (Disk Read), and 2) writing data equal to the RAM size to keep all data in RAM (Cached Read). Table 3 compares these two modes.

The Cached Read experiment performed more than 2100 requests/s (104 MB/s reaching 79% network bandwidth) matching the maximum write throughput (Section 6.1.3), compared to 540 requests/s for Disk Reads. We also measured the average, maximum, minimum, and standard deviation of latency, shown in Table 3. In both cases, the minimum is equal to reading from the Linux Cache. However, the Cached Read case improves the average and max latency by 5.5x and 13x, respectively. This shows the significance of exploiting the Linux Cache (Section 4.3.2).

| | Average | Max | Min | StdDev |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Disk Reads | 17 ms | 67 ms | 1.6 ms | 9 ms |
| Cached Reads | 3 ms | 5 ms | 1.6 ms | 0.4 ms |
| Improvement | 5.5x | 13x | 0 | 23x |

*Table 3: Comparision of get request latency when most of requests (83%) are served by disk (Disk Reads) and when all requests are served by linux cache (Cached Reads) for 50 KB blobs.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们使用 50 KB blob 和 2 个 client，在两种配置下运行 micro-benchmark：1）读取前写入相当于 RAM 容量 6 倍的数据，使大多数请求（83%）由磁盘提供服务（Disk Read）；2）写入与 RAM 容量相等的数据，使所有数据都保留在 RAM 中（Cached Read）。表 3 对比了这两种模式。

Cached Read 实验达到超过 2100 requests/s（104 MB/s，相当于网络带宽的 79%），与最大写吞吐量相当（第 6.1.3 节）；Disk Reads 则为 540 requests/s。我们还测量了延迟的平均值、最大值、最小值和标准差，如表 3 所示。在两种情况下，最小延迟都等同于从 Linux Cache 读取的延迟。然而，Cached Read 分别将平均延迟和最大延迟改善了 5.5 倍和 13 倍。这说明利用 Linux Cache 十分重要（第 4.3.2 节）。

| | 平均值 | 最大值 | 最小值 | 标准差 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Disk Reads | 17 ms | 67 ms | 1.6 ms | 9 ms |
| Cached Reads | 3 ms | 5 ms | 1.6 ms | 0.4 ms |
| 改善倍数 | 5.5x | 13x | 0 | 23x |

*表 3：对于 50 KB blob，对比大多数请求（83%）由磁盘提供服务（Disk Reads）和所有请求均由 linux cache 提供服务（Cached Reads）时 get 请求的延迟。*

sections.title: 6.2 Geo-distributed Optimizations
sections.originalMarkdown: We analyzed our replication algorithm among 3 different datacenters at LinkedIn {DC1, DC2, DC3}, located all across the US. All experiments in this section are from production workloads.
sections.translationMarkdown: 我们分析了复制算法在 LinkedIn 位于美国各地的 3 个不同 datacenter {DC1, DC2, DC3} 之间的表现。本节所有实验均基于生产 workload。

sections.title: 6.2.1 Replication Lag
sections.originalMarkdown:
We define replication lag between a pair of replicas (r1, r2) as the difference of r2’s highest used offset and r1’s latest synced offset with r2. Note that not all data in the replication lag needs to be transfered to r1, since it could receive the missing blobs from other replicas as well.

We measured the replication lag among all replicas of a given Datanode and the rest of the cluster, and found that more than 85% of the values were 0. Figure 11 shows the CDF of non-zero values grouped by datacenter. The 95th percentile is less than 1 KB for 100 GB partitions (in all datacenters), with slightly worse lag in datacenter 3 since it is placed farther apart from others.

*Figure 11: CDF of Replication Lag among replica pairs of a given Datanode and the rest of the cluster. Most values were zero, and are omitted from the graph.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们将一对副本（r1, r2）之间的 replication lag 定义为 r2 已使用的最高 offset 与 r1 最近一次同 r2 同步的 offset 之间的差值。需要注意的是，replication lag 中并非所有数据都需要传输到 r1，因为 r1 也可能从其他副本接收到缺失的 blob。

我们测量了给定 Datanode 的所有副本与集群其余副本之间的 replication lag，发现超过 85% 的值为 0。图 11 展示了按 datacenter 分组的非零值 CDF。对于 100 GB partition，所有 datacenter 的第 95 百分位均小于 1 KB；由于 datacenter 3 与其他 datacenter 距离更远，其滞后情况略差。

*图 11：给定 Datanode 与集群其余部分的副本对之间 Replication Lag 的 CDF。大多数值为零，因此未在图中显示。*

sections.title: 6.2.2 Replication Bandwidth
sections.originalMarkdown:
Ambry relies on background replication to write data to other datacenters. We measured the aggregate network bandwidth used for inter-datacenter replication during a 24 hour period, shown in Figure 12. The aggregate bandwidth is small (< 10 MB/s), similar across all datacenters, and correlated to request rates with a diurnal pattern. This value is small because we batch replication between common replicas and due to the read-heavy nature of the workload.

Figure 13 demonstrates the CDF of average replication bandwidth per Datanode, for both intra- and inter-datacenter replication. Intra-datacenter bandwidth is minimal (< 200 B/s at 95th percentile), especially compared to inter-datacenter with 150-200 KB/s at 95th percentile (1000x larger).

The higher value for inter-datacenter is because of asynchronous writes. However, the inter-datacenter bandwidth is still negligible (≈ 0.2% of a 1 Gb/s link). The small difference among the 3 datacenters is due to the different request rates they receive.

Figure 14 shows a zoomed in graph of only inter-datacenter bandwidth. Inter-datacenter replication has a short tail with the 95th to 5th percentile ratio of about 3x. This short tail is because of the load-balanced design in Ambry. Intra-datacenter replication has a longer tail, as well as many zero values (omitted from the graph). Thus, replication either uses almost zero bandwidth (intra-datacenter) or almost balanced bandwidth (inter-datacenter).

*Figure 12: Aggregate network bandwidth used for inter-datacenter replication during a 24 hour period in production.*

*Figure 13: CDF of average network bandwidth used per Datanode for intra- and inter-datacenter replication over a 24 hour period in production.*

*Figure 14: CDF of average inter-datacenter network bandwidth used per Datanode over a 24 hour period in production.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
Ambry 依赖后台复制将数据写入其他 datacenter。我们测量了生产环境中 24 小时内 datacenter 间复制使用的聚合网络带宽，如图 12 所示。聚合带宽较小（< 10 MB/s），各 datacenter 之间相近，并且与请求速率相关，呈现昼夜周期模式。该值较小，是因为我们会对共有副本间的复制进行 batching，也因为 workload 以读为主。

图 13 展示了 datacenter 内和 datacenter 间复制中，每个 Datanode 平均复制带宽的 CDF。datacenter 内带宽极小（第 95 百分位 < 200 B/s），尤其是与 datacenter 间复制相比，后者第 95 百分位为 150–200 KB/s（大 1000 倍）。

datacenter 间的数值更高是由异步写入造成的。不过，datacenter 间带宽仍可忽略不计（约为 1 Gb/s 链路的 0.2%）。3 个 datacenter 之间的微小差异源于它们接收的请求速率不同。

图 14 仅对 datacenter 间带宽进行了放大展示。datacenter 间复制的尾部很短，第 95 百分位与第 5 百分位之比约为 3 倍。短尾来自 Ambry 的负载均衡设计。datacenter 内复制的尾部更长，并且存在大量零值（图中省略）。因此，复制要么几乎不使用带宽（datacenter 内），要么使用近乎均衡的带宽（datacenter 间）。

*图 12：生产环境中 24 小时内，datacenter 间复制所使用的聚合网络带宽。*

*图 13：生产环境中 24 小时内，datacenter 内和 datacenter 间复制所使用的每 Datanode 平均网络带宽的 CDF。*

*图 14：生产环境中 24 小时内，每个 Datanode 所使用的 datacenter 间平均网络带宽的 CDF。*

sections.title: 6.2.3 Replication Latency
sections.originalMarkdown:
We define replication latency as the time spent in one iteration of the replication protocol, i.e., $T_{missing\ blobs\ received}$ minus $T_{replication\ initiated}$. Figure 15 demonstrates the CDF of average replication latency for intra- and inter-datacenter, in our production workload.

Inter-datacenter replication latency is low with a median of less than 150 ms, and a very short tail. Although this latency might appear to be high, the number of proxy requests remain near-zero (< 0.001%). This is because users usually read data from the same local datacenter to which they have recently written. Therefore, replication has a minimal effect on user experience.

Surprisingly, intra-datacenter replication latency is relatively high (6x more than inter-datacenter) and with little variance. This pattern is because of a pre-existing and prefixed artificial added delay of 1 second, intended to prevent incorrect blob collision detections. If a blob is replicated in a datacenter faster than the Datanode receives the initial put request (which is possible with less strict policies), the Datanode might consider the put as a blob collision and fail the request. The artificial delay is used to prevent this issue in intra-datacenter replication. This relatively small delay does not have a significant impact on durability or availability of data since intra-replication is only used to fix failed/slow Datanodes, which rarely occurs.

*Figure 15: CDF of average replication latency (i.e., time spent to recieve missing blobs) for intra- and inter-datacenter in production environment.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们将 replication latency 定义为复制协议一次迭代所花费的时间，即 $T_{missing\ blobs\ received}$ 减去 $T_{replication\ initiated}$。图 15 展示了生产 workload 下 datacenter 内和 datacenter 间平均复制延迟的 CDF。

datacenter 间复制延迟较低，中位数小于 150 ms，且尾部很短。虽然这一延迟看起来可能较高，但 proxy 请求数仍接近于零（< 0.001%）。这是因为用户通常会从其最近写入数据的同一个本地 datacenter 读取数据。因此，复制对用户体验的影响极小。

令人意外的是，datacenter 内复制延迟相对较高（是 datacenter 间复制的 6 倍），并且方差很小。这种模式是由一个预先存在、预先设定的 1 秒人为附加延迟造成的，该延迟旨在防止错误检测 blob collision。如果某个 blob 在 datacenter 内完成复制的速度快于 Datanode 接收最初 put 请求的速度（在约束较宽松的策略下可能发生），Datanode 可能会将该 put 视为 blob collision，并使请求失败。datacenter 内复制使用人为延迟来避免这一问题。由于 intra-replication 仅用于修复很少出现的故障或缓慢 Datanode，这一相对较小的延迟不会显著影响数据的持久性或可用性。

*图 15：生产环境中，datacenter 内和 datacenter 间平均复制延迟（即接收缺失 blob 所花费的时间）的 CDF。*

sections.title: 6.3 Load Balancing
sections.originalMarkdown: Since cluster growth occurs infrequently (every few months at most), we implemented a simulator to show the behavior of Ambry over a long period of time (several years), and at large scale (hundreds of Datanodes). We tried a workload that is based on production workloads. All results in this section are gathered using the simulator.
sections.translationMarkdown: 由于集群扩容并不频繁（最多每几个月一次），我们实现了一个 simulator，用于展示 Ambry 在长时间跨度（数年）和大规模（数百个 Datanode）下的行为。我们采用了基于生产 workload 的 workload。本节所有结果均通过该 simulator 获得。

sections.title: 6.3.1 Simulator Design
sections.originalMarkdown:
The simulator’s design resembles Ambry’s architecture and requests are served using the same path. However, there are no real physical disks. For handling requests, only the metadata (blob id and size) is stored/retrieved, and the effect of requests are reflected (e.g., disk space increase).

**Workload:** We use a synthetic workload closely resembling the real-world traffic at LinkedIn. This workload preserves the rate of each type of request (read, write, and delete), the blob size distribution, and the access pattern of blobs (based on age).

**Cluster Expansion:** The simulator starts with an initial set of Datanodes, disks, and partitions in one datacenter. Over time, when partitions reach the capacity threshold, a new batch of partitions are added using the replica placement strategy from Section 2.2. If partitions cannot be added (e.g., if there is not enough unallocated space on disks), a batch of new Datanodes are added.
sections.translationMarkdown:
Simulator 的设计与 Ambry 的架构相似，请求也通过相同路径得到处理。不过，其中没有真实的物理磁盘。处理请求时，只存储或检索 metadata（blob id 和大小），并反映请求带来的影响（例如磁盘空间增加）。

**Workload：** 我们使用与 LinkedIn 真实流量高度相似的合成 workload。该 workload 保留了各类请求（读、写和删除）的速率、blob 大小分布以及 blob 的访问模式（基于年龄）。

**集群扩容：** Simulator 从一个 datacenter 中的一组初始 Datanode、磁盘和 partition 开始。随着时间推移，当 partition 达到容量阈值时，使用第 2.2 节的副本放置策略添加一批新 partition。如果无法继续添加 partition（例如磁盘上没有足够的未分配空间），则添加一批新 Datanode。

sections.title: 6.3.2 Experiment Setup
sections.originalMarkdown: The simulation is run in a single datacenter, with 10 Frontend nodes. The experiment starts with 15 Datanodes, each with 10 4TB disks, and 1200 100GB partitions with 3 replicas. At each partition and Datanode addition point, a batch of 600 partitions and 15 Datanodes are added, respectively. The simulation is run over 400 weeks (almost 8 years) and up to 240 Datanodes. The simulation is run with and without rebalancing with the exact same configuration, while measuring request rates, disk usage, and data movement.
sections.translationMarkdown: 模拟在单个 datacenter 中运行，其中包含 10 个 Frontend 节点。实验开始时有 15 个 Datanode，每个 Datanode 配有 10 块 4 TB 磁盘；系统包含 1200 个 100 GB partition，每个 partition 有 3 个副本。每到一个 partition 或 Datanode 添加点，分别批量添加 600 个 partition 和 15 个 Datanode。模拟持续运行 400 周（接近 8 年），Datanode 最多扩展到 240 个。我们在完全相同的配置下分别运行启用和未启用 rebalancing 的模拟，同时测量请求速率、磁盘使用量和数据移动量。

sections.title: 6.3.3 Request Rate
sections.originalMarkdown:
We measured the read rate (KB/s) for each disk at any point of time. Figure 16 demonstrates the average, standard deviation, maximum and minimum among these values, for the system with and without rebalancing. The results for write rates were similar and removed due to lack of space.

The average, which is also the ideal, is a dropping step function. The drops are points where new Datanodes were added to the cluster. In case of no rebalancing, a majority of the disks are old read-only disks with almost zero traffic, while a few disks receive most of the request. Thus, the minimum is close to zero. Also, the maximum and standard deviation are significant (3x-7x and 1x-2x larger than the average, respectively). When rebalancing is added, the minimum and maximum move close to the average, and the standard deviation drops close to zero. We conclude that Ambry’s load balancing is effective.

*Figure 16: Average, standard deviation, maximum and minimum of average read rate (KB/s) among disks over a 400 week interval. The system is bootstrapping in the first few weeks, and the results are omitted.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们测量了任意时刻每块磁盘的读速率（KB/s）。图 16 展示了启用和未启用 rebalancing 时，这些数值的平均值、标准差、最大值和最小值。写速率的结果与此类似，因篇幅有限而省略。

平均值也是理想值，它呈现为一个不断下降的阶梯函数。每次下降都对应向集群添加新 Datanode 的时刻。在未启用 rebalancing 的情况下，大多数磁盘是流量接近于零的旧只读磁盘，而少数磁盘接收了大部分请求。因此，最小值接近于零。此外，最大值和标准差都很大，分别比平均值高 3–7 倍和 1–2 倍。启用 rebalancing 后，最小值和最大值都接近平均值，标准差也降至接近于零。由此可见，Ambry 的负载均衡是有效的。

*图 16：在 400 周区间内，各磁盘平均读速率（KB/s）的平均值、标准差、最大值和最小值。系统在最初几周处于启动阶段，因此省略了这些结果。*

sections.title: 6.3.4 Disk Usage
sections.originalMarkdown:
We analyzed the disk usage ratio, i.e., used space divided by total space among disks, with and without rebalancing. As seen in Figure 17, without rebalancing, the maximum stays at the capacity threshold since some disks become and remain full, while the minimum drops to zero whenever new Datanodes are added. With rebalancing, the maximum and minimum move closer to the average with temporary drops in the minimum until rebalancing is completed. Additionally, the standard deviation drops significantly, becoming almost zero with temporary spikes on Datanode addition points.

*Figure 17: Average, standard deviation, maximum and minimum of disk usage ratio (i.e., used space divided by total space) over a 400 week interval.*
sections.translationMarkdown:
我们分析了启用和未启用 rebalancing 时，各磁盘的磁盘使用率，即已用空间除以总空间。如图 17 所示，在未启用 rebalancing 时，由于部分磁盘达到并一直保持满载，最大值维持在容量阈值；而每当添加新 Datanode 时，最小值都会降至零。启用 rebalancing 后，最大值和最小值都向平均值靠近；在 rebalancing 完成之前，最小值会暂时下降。此外，标准差显著下降至接近于零，仅在添加 Datanode 的时刻出现短暂尖峰。

*图 17：400 周区间内磁盘使用率（即已用空间除以总空间）的平均值、标准差、最大值和最小值。*

sections.title: 6.3.5 Evaluation Over Time
sections.originalMarkdown:
We evaluated the improvement over time by measuring the integral of range (max-min) and standard deviation for request rate and disk usage over the 400 week interval. As shown in Table 4, rebalancing has a prominent effect improving the range and standard deviation by 6x-9x and 8x-10x, respectively.

| Integral over 400 weeks | Write Avg | Read Avg | Disk Usage |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Range w/o RB | 63,000 | 340,000 | 200 |
| Range w/ RB | 8,500 | 52,000 | 22 |
| Improvement | 7.5x | 6x | 9x |
| StdDev w/o RB | 21,00 | 112,000 | 67 |
| StdDev w/ RB | 2500 | 11,000 | 6.7 |
| Improvement | 8x | 10x | 10x |

*Table 4: Improvement of range (max-min) and standard deviation of request rates and disk usage over a 400 week interval. Results are from the system with rebalancing (w/ RB) and without rebalancing (w/o RB).*
